title: rubus radula subsp. timendus
intro: Rubus radula subsp. timendus, also known as the Tasmanian raspberry or wild raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to Tasmania. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus radula subsp. timendus include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and support the immune system. It can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, to utilize its medicinal properties.
